summary: Completed frontend testing of the Leadership Architects /team page. All 15 required sections render with correct data-testids, required content is present verbatim (founder quote, governance disclaimer, headline, CTAs), counts are correct (3 philosophy cards, 8 architecture domains, 3 board, 5 executive, 6 leader profiles with working expand/collapse, 9 operating model stages, 8x8 ownership matrix, 8 credibility cards, 8 future advisor placeholders), DEXA uses abstract intelligence core visual (no human/robot), navbar Team link navigates to /team and scrolls to top, and all 10 regression pages return 200 with zero console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Team.jsx is 1360+ lines — consider splitting each section into /app/frontend/src/components/team/*.jsx for maintainability (File Size guideline >700 lines).

Minor: /solutions/healthcare, /solutions/fintech, /solutions/ai subroutes returned a very short body (~932 chars) during regression — may indicate near-empty pages; pre-existing, unrelated to /team task, but worth E1 verifying those routes render intended content.
architectureDomains slicing in Hero (.slice(1,7)) correctly picks 6 human architects between founder (0) and DEXA (7) — OK.
Leader cards expand/collapse state is per-card (local useState). Works correctly; confirmed via Playwright click on expand-navroz which revealed Infrastructure Pillars + Show Less button.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Team page fully verified. Sections rendered via data-testid selectors: team-page, team-hero, team-founder-quote, team-philosophy, team-architecture-map, team-founder-feature, team-board, team-executive, team-dexa, team-profiles, team-operating-model, team-ownership-matrix, team-credibility, team-governance-note, team-future-advisors, team-final-cta. Leader cards expose leader-card-{id} and expand-{id}. Three photos are real (Dr. Ali, Navroz, Khubaib); four have 'Photo Coming Soon' placeholders (Rafique, Jawwad, Sarmad, Ovais). DEXA uses abstract SVG intelligence core (no human/robot). When asserting eyebrows or uppercase headings in Playwright, use case-insensitive matching because CSS text-transform:uppercase affects inner_text().
